ui.pageIntro: Five small doors from Maundy Thursday through Easter Monday.
ui.statusText: {open} of {total} doors are open right now.
ui.footerText: Built as a simple GitHub Pages version with no dependencies.

maundyThursday.title: Maundy Thursday
maundyThursday.body: A quiet moment. Notice one small thing that feels kind today and let that be enough. You can replace this text with anything: a poem, an image, an audio link, or a tiny prompt.

goodFriday.title: Good Friday
goodFriday.body: In a light version, each door can stay very simple. One text, one image, or one link is usually enough. The goal is low friction and easy maintenance.

holySaturday.title: Holy Saturday
holySaturday.body: This is placeholder text. You can replace it with something personal, playful, or quiet. The calendar works without extra libraries and can be published directly on GitHub Pages.

easterSunday.title: Easter Sunday
easterSunday.body: Here you can add a short greeting, a quote, an image, or a brief audio file by link. Later we can make each door more visual if you want.

easterMonday.title: Easter Monday
easterMonday.body: Final door in the light version. The structure is designed to resemble your original project with separate folders for images, languages, flags, and reminders.
